MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Asbestos Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee; Clerk: J. Bertelsen; Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.
Janine M. Fiel-Cosse, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Dennis King.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendants Dalziel Supply Company or Lorentzen Co.
Default of Defendants has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendants as follows:
IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages as follows:
From defendant Dalziel Supply Company, in the amount of $80,000.00 in Economic Damages, $750,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $830,000.00.
From defendant Lorentzen Co., in the amount of $80,000.00 in Economic Damages, $750,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $830,000.00.
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

Notice Of Continuance Of Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Defendant Black Swan Manufacturing Co. To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And To Produce Documents At Deposition
Off Calendar May-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Meeti S. Sudame of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Dennis King

RULING:
PLAINTIFF DENNIS KING'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT BLACK SWAN MANUFACTURING CO. TO PRODUCE ITS CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS AND PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE FOR DEPOSITION AND TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS AT DEPOSITION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to compel defendant Black Swan Manufacturing Co. to produce its custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and to produce documents at deposition is granted. Plaintiff properly noticed defendant's deposition, defendant did not file objections, defendant did not substantively oppose this motion nor appear at the initially scheduled hearing date and there has been no demonstration why this Court should not issue an order compelling the deposition and document production. The deposition must commence no later than June 6, 2014 with documents to be produced at the deposition. Plaintiff's request for sanctions of $1,061 is granted. (C.C.P. 2025.450(g).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
